content.problemStatement: Read problems statements in Russian here The Head Chef has received his id from the Association of Byteland . He wants to know the numerical rank of his number among the numbers that can be formed by the exact same digits ( i.e. among numbers having same number of 0's , 1's ... 9's as his id ) .  Note that id's can start with 0 . Given a number, find the rank of the number .
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the rank of the number.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The numbers that can be formed with one '2'; one '7' and one '6' in increasing order are: 267, 276, 627, 672, 726, 762. The rank for 276 is 2 and rank for 762 is 6.
The only number that can be formed with one '1', one '2', and one '3' is 123 itself, so its rank is 1.
The numbers that can be formed with one '3', one '2', and one '1' in increasing order are: 123, 132, 213, 231, 312, 321. The rank for 321 is 6.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by calculating the rank of the number among all possible permutations of its digits. This can be done by iterating over each digit in the number and calculating the number of permutations that would come before it. The factorial of the remaining digits is used to calculate the number of permutations.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) per test case, O(T * N) total. Precomputation is not required.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the frequency of each digit.
